key_insight: A hierarchical stability-screening pipeline can convert many machine-learning-predicted materials into a much smaller set of experimentally actionable synthesis targets.
engineering_relevance: The framework helps materials engineers prioritize candidates with the highest chance of real-world synthesis by filtering for thermodynamic and dynamical robustness across increasingly realistic criteria.
method: Start with 894 previously reported stable compounds and curate 603 unique structures.
Screen with complete PBE phase diagrams, then apply phonon and finite-temperature MD dynamical stability checks using machine-learning interatomic potentials.
Refine surviving candidates with SCAN phase diagrams and rank by decomposition enthalpy plus chemical-space completeness.
evidence_signal: From 603 curated structures, the pipeline narrows the set to 298 PBE-stable, 166 dynamically stable, 109 SCAN-stable, and finally 25 high-confidence synthesis targets.
watch_item: Stability screening still depends on the accuracy of the underlying phase diagrams and machine-learning potentials, so some promising candidates may be filtered out or misranked.
paper_title: Predicting Novel Stable Materials for Experimental Synthesis
